utility_evaluation.REASONING: The required format has 5 top-level items: health_coverage, property_coverage, travel_coverage, additional_services, total_budget. Now check the final plan: it includes health_coverage, property_coverage, travel_coverage, additional_services, and total_budget — all 5 appear. Next, verify if each is fully specified with costs as required. Health_coverage: includes physical and mental health insurance with explicit monthly costs and a total health cost — fully specified. Property_coverage: includes home/contents insurance (contents) with costs and add-on, plus total property cost — sufficiently specified with costs. Travel_coverage: includes travel insurance entry but provides only plan name and coverage type; it lacks an explicit cost requirement? The item says 'travel insurance with costs' — the plan lists 'LifeFirst Standard Plan - €50/month' under travel_insurance; that is a cost, so this is specified. Additional_services: includes services but the format requires 'any enhancement services with costs' — the plan lists services but no costs (many marked 'Included' without monetary amounts). This may not meet 'with costs' strictly; 'Included' could be interpreted as €0 cost, but no explicit cost values are given. Therefore, additional_services is not fully specified according to the requirement. Total_budget: provides monthly and annual cost breakdown — fully specified. So, 4 of 5 items are fully specified.

llm_ratings_evaluation.REASONING: The final coverage plan includes specific items by name. I matched each named plan/add-on to the exact corresponding entry in the ground-truth ratings: LifeFirst Standard Plan appears under physical health (rating 9), mental health (rating 9), and travel insurance (rating 9). LifeFirst Standard Coverage appears under home content/property protection (rating 9). The Adventure Equipment Add-on is not present in the ground-truth list, so it cannot be mapped and is omitted. Other described services like telemedicine, gym reimbursement, and 24/7 support are included as features but not listed as separately rated items in the ground-truth; thus, they are not mapped. Therefore, the items that can be mapped are the three LifeFirst Standard Plan entries and the LifeFirst Standard Coverage entry.
